basics.name: Abdalkarim Akilan
basics.label: Staff Software Engineer
basics.summary: Staff engineer, 12+ years shipping web products and 7+ in fintech. Build platforms and B2B products end-to-end with React, TypeScript, and Node.js, with recent work taking LLM-powered tools to production.

work.name: Careem
work.description: Careem Pay is the leading fintech player in the region.

highlights: Drive AI-tooling adoption across a 500-person engineering org: produced weekly internal Claude Code videos and built Careem's internal Claude Code marketplace where engineers publish and share skills.
Embedded a natural-language assistant (OpenAI) inside the disbursement portal so partners can check balances, navigate, and execute payouts in chat.

Pioneered Super Portal, a microfrontend platform built on native import maps and Vite-plugin tooling, consolidating 25+ Careem web apps across 10+ teams. Serves ~3k daily users and cut new-app launch from weeks to days.
Designed and built the disbursement portal: a B2B payout product serving 20+ partner enterprises (multi-million GMV) with role-based multi-factor approval flows for payout batches.
Shipped Careem Checkout 0 to 1: hosted web app, merchant SDK, and Shopify plugin for embedding payments into third-party surfaces.

work.name: Majid Al Futtaim
work.description: Leading retail and leisure pioneer across MEA and Asia.
work.summary: Built the merchant-facing portal for MAF Payments, the group's in-house payment gateway.
highlights: Owned end-to-end the merchant business portal for MAF Payments (Angular): admin and merchant tooling for the group's in-house payment gateway.
Designed the role and permission model spanning merchant users and internal administrators across the gateway's operational surfaces.

work.name: Mondia
work.summary: Built VAS web portals deployed as carrier landing pages across du, Vodafone Egypt, Ooredoo, Omantel, and other regional operators.
highlights: Proposed and led migration off legacy Liferay portlets to a modern Angular SPA backed by a headless CMS; became the framework new operator portals were built on.
Built and shipped carrier VAS portals and music products for du, Etisalat Egypt (music), Omantel, and Ooredoo, including an in-browser music player that headlined several launches.

education.institution: American University of Sharjah | Sharjah, United Arab Emirates
